foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Couinaud segmental anatomy determines resectability; understanding hepatic vein and portal pedicle anatomy is essential for surgical planning

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: HCC almost always arises in cirrhotic livers with portal hypertension; HVPG >10mmHg contraindicates major hepatic resection (BCLC criteria)

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Key differential diagnosis; multiphase CT/MRI arterial enhancement pattern distinguishes HCC from metastatic deposits

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Necrotic HCC can mimic liver abscess on imaging; AFP and contrast enhancement pattern help differentiate

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Milan criteria (single ≤5cm or up to 3 lesions ≤3cm) define transplant eligibility for HCC with underlying cirrhosis

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Large or hilar HCC can cause biliary obstruction; jaundice in HCC indicates advanced disease and poor prognosis

objectives: SU28.10: Describe the applied anatomy of liver. Describe the Clinical features,
IM5.6: Describe and discuss the pathophysiology, clinical evolution and
integration: This undergraduate-level roadmap connects Hepatocellular Carcinoma across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
